entries.title: Comey charged with threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
entries.snippet: The new case stems from a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director that the justice department says calls for violence against Trump.

entries.title: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
entries.snippet: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

entries.title: Musk says basis of charitable giving at stake in OpenAI lawsuit
entries.snippet: The case over OpenAI's history and public commitments could have major implications for the future of AI.

entries.title: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
entries.snippet: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

entries.title: US regulator to review Disney broadcast licences after Jimmy Kimmel joke about Melania Trump
entries.snippet: The move comes as the White House pressures Disney-owned ABC to fire Kimmel after he called Melania an "expectant widow".

entries.title: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
entries.snippet: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

entries.title: Path cleared for Everest climbers after huge ice block
entries.snippet: Experts warn there are still risks of further ice collapses, and there are fears climbers will again have to queue to reach the summit.

entries.title: Ex-TV showgirl's pardon at centre of widening Italian scandal
entries.snippet: Nicole Minetti was convicted of facilitating prostitution in connection with the notorious "bunga bunga" parties at Silvio Berlusconi's villa.

entries.title: Suspected gunman aged 89 held in Greece after five wounded
entries.snippet: Five people were left with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Greece's state broadcaster ERT.

entries.title: Ukraine accuses Israel of receiving shipments of grain 'stolen' by Russia
entries.snippet: Israel's foreign minister says it has received no evidence to support the claim that a vessel carrying grain from occupied Ukraine has reached Haifa.

entries.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
entries.snippet: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

entries.title: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump at Washington dinner
entries.snippet: Investigators say the 31-year-old California man wanted to kill as many high-level officials as possible.

entries.title: Man admits plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
entries.snippet: The state prosecutor told the court that police found an almost completed bomb in a search of the man's house.

entries.title: Rescuers race to free survivors trapped inside train after fatal Indonesia crash
entries.snippet: Two trains collided on the outskirts of the Indonesian capital on Monday, killing at least 15 people.

entries.title: Ex-actor Nathan Chasing Horse jailed for at least 37 years for sexual assault
entries.snippet: The 49-year-old exploited his position as a spiritual leader to prey on Indigenous women and girls, the US court heard.

entries.title: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
entries.snippet: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

entries.title: IS says it was behind deadly Nigeria attack on football pitch
entries.snippet: Authorities say the militants raided a football pitch where people had been gathering and opened fire at random.

entries.title: Venice opera house drops incoming music director after nepotism remarks
entries.snippet: Beatrice Venezi's appointment had been marred in controversy since being announced last September, prompting orchestra members to go on strike.

entries.title: Pakistan accused of attacking Afghan university
entries.snippet: Air attacks on the province of Kunar have killed at least seven people and injured 75, sources tell the BBC.

entries.title: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
entries.snippet: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

entries.title: Yomif Kejelcha broke the 2-hour marathon but got 2nd place. He's still happy
entries.snippet: Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha ran the London Marathon in under two hours, but he only got second place. He told NPR he hopes to run his next marathon a minute faster.

entries.title: Scientists see Trump's firing of the National Science Board as an attack on research
entries.snippet: The move follows an administration push for cuts to the NSF and raises concerns in the scientific community that it could jeopardize a tradition of independent decisions about federal science grants.

entries.title: Linda McMahon punches back at senators questioning Education Department cuts
entries.snippet: In her first appearance on Capitol Hill this year, lawmakers questioned Education Secretary Linda McMahon about students' civil rights and cuts to federal education spending.

entries.title: With no radical footprint, what drove suspect to try and assassinate Trump?
entries.snippet: An attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner on Saturday has, again, highlighted the climate of political violence in the U.S. But there are still many questions about the motive.

entries.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
entries.snippet: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

entries.title: Grand jury indicts former FBI director James Comey for a second time
entries.snippet: The case revolves around a photo the former FBI director posted online last year of seashells on a beach arranged to say "8647."

entries.title: Elon Musk testifies against OpenAI, seeking Sam Altman's ouster
entries.snippet: Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."

entries.title: Trump's 'American flag blue' reflecting pool project gets a mixed reaction in D.C.
entries.snippet: The pool is being resurfaced in a shade more akin to that of a swimming pool. It's one of many physical changes Trump is planning for the nation's capital.

entries.title: South Korean court extends prison sentence for wife of ousted president
entries.snippet: In January, Kim Keon Hee was sentenced to 20 months for accepting gifts from the Unification Church, which sought political favors.

entries.title: Natural disasters can cause another crisis for those recovering from opioid addiction
entries.snippet: People recovering from opioid addiction risk relapse when they can't get their medications after natural disasters. A group of doctors is calling for lawmakers to ease access to the meds.

entries.title: Senate Republicans reject attempt to end Trump's blockade of Cuba
entries.snippet: The vote on the war powers resolution showed how Republicans continue to stand behind Trump as he acts unilaterally to exert American force in a range of global conflicts, including Venezuela, Iran and Cuba.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: Fed Chair Powell holds briefing on interest rate decision as his term nears end
entries.snippet: The Federal Reserve will meet this week ahead of a looming leadership transition that remains fuzzy, and Wednesday's news conference will be closely watched for any clarification.

entries.title: Actor Nathan Chasing Horse sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault
entries.snippet: A Nevada judge sentenced "Dances With Wolves" actor Nathan Chasing Horse on Monday to life in prison for sexually assaulting Indigenous women and girls. A jury had previously convicted him of 13 charges, mostly related to sexual assault of three women.

entries.title: James Comey indicted over social media post Trump's DOJ says crossed a line
entries.snippet: Former FBI director James Comey has once again been indicted by the Department of Justice. It's the second time the Trump administration has attempted to prosecute him. The latest is connected to a post Comey shared last year of seashells spelling out the numbers "86 47." The DOJ

entries.title: How the UAE's departure from OPEC could impact oil markets
entries.snippet: There seems to be little movement on the resumption of talks between the U.S. and Iran. There is also almost no movement through the Strait of Hormuz, the vital bottleneck that's essentially cut off 20% of the world's oil and gas supply. Tuesday, the UAE announced it will leave O

entries.title: Jack Thornell, Pulitzer-winning photographer of Civil Rights Movement, dies at 86
entries.snippet: Former Associated Press photographer Jack Thornell, whose Pulitzer Prize-winning picture of a shotgun-felled James Meredith looking back toward his would-be assassin on a Mississippi highway in 1966 became an enduring image of the Civil Rights Movement, has died.

entries.title: King Charles calls for unity in rare royal address to Congress
entries.snippet: The king and queen of England were received with high diplomatic fanfare at the White House on day two of the royal visit. King Charles became just the second monarch to address Congress. He delivered a message of unity and peace during a time of tense relations. Nick Schifrin re

entries.title: News Wrap: Federal agents search Minnesota childcare centers
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, federal agents carried search warrants at childcare centers in Minnesota, a former advisor to Dr. Anthony Fauci at the National Institutes of Health has been indicted by a federal grand jury in Maryland for conspiring to hide records related to the pande

entries.title: FCC threatens ABC's licenses as Trumps call for Kimmel's firing
entries.snippet: The FCC is ramping up the pressure on ABC and Disney by threatening to strip broadcasters of their station licenses. The FCC says the review of the licenses is tied to Disney's DEI initiatives, but it's widely seen as retaliation in the ongoing battle between President Trump and

entries.title: The mental health toll and lifelong consequences of gun violence in America
entries.snippet: Gun violence continues to impact communities across the country daily. Many don't make the news, but create lifelong consequences for victims, their families and even entire neighborhoods. The normalization of shootings has created a persistent public health crisis. William Brang

entries.title: WATCH: Justice Department holds news briefing on Comey indictment over '86 47' photo
entries.snippet: Former FBI Director James Comey was indicted again Tuesday, this time in an investigation over a social media photo of seashells arranged on a beach that officials said constituted a threat against President Donald Trump.

entries.title: Families sound alarm on toxic mold in military housing
entries.snippet: Military families are sounding the alarm about the presence of toxic mold in military housing. It's an issue that has long been making service members and their families sick. Stephanie Sy reports on the ongoing problem and what is and isn't being done to address it.

entries.title: Tessa Thompson and Adrien Brody on Broadway debuts in 'The Fear of 13'
entries.snippet: In 2004, Nick Yarris walked out of a Pennsylvania prison after 22 years on death row. His was the first death row case in Pennsylvania overturned by DNA evidence. His wrongful conviction is now the focus of the new play "The Fear of 13." It stars Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson i

entries.title: Students injured in Brown University shooting sue school over alleged security failures
entries.snippet: Three students who were injured in the December campus shooting at Brown University are each suing the Ivy League school, alleging it ignored prior warnings about the shooter and did not provide adequate security that could have prevented the tragedy.

entries.title: U.S. soldier pleads not guilty to using Maduro raid intel to win $400,000 on Polymarket
entries.snippet: A U.S. special forces soldier pleaded not guilty Tuesday to charges that he used classified information about the mission to capture former Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro to win more than $400,000 on the prediction market Polymarket.

entries.title: Starmer avoids ethics probe over Mandelson appointment but challenges lie ahead
entries.snippet: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Tuesday averted a parliamentary inquiry over his choice of Peter Mandelson as British ambassador to Washington, but failed to quell questions about whether he bent the rules to make the controversial appointment.

entries.title: James Comey indicted in probe over online post officials say constituted Trump threat
entries.snippet: The criminal case is the second in a matter of months against Comey and is part of the Trump administration Justice Department's relentless effort to prosecute political opponents of the Republican president.

entries.title: Taylor Swift files new trademark applications. One expert says it is to curb AI threats
entries.snippet: Swift has been a target of AI misuse in the past.

entries.title: Justice Department accuses former Fauci adviser of concealing communications related to COVID research
entries.snippet: A former senior adviser to Dr. Anthony Fauci has been indicted on federal charges alleging he conspired to hide his communications related to COVID-19 research as the pandemic unfolded.

entries.title: Ex-player Damon Jones first to plead guilty in basketball gambling sweep
entries.snippet: Former NBA player and assistant coach Damon Jones has become the first person to plead guilty in a gambling sweep that led to the arrests of more than 30 people.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: EU Parliament approves common definition of rape
entries.snippet: Until now, definitions of what constitutes rape under criminal law had varied significantly across the EU, ranging from "only yes means yes" to "only no means no."

entries.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
entries.snippet: A US backed gas pipeline could help Bosnia cut reliance on Russian energy. But Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could put the country's EU hopes at risk.

entries.title: Venezuela: German entrepreneurs bet on business boom
entries.snippet: Venezuela was once a key destination for German industry. However, after nearly 30 years of socialism, only a handful of German companies remain active there. That could change if the nation opens up to foreign firms.

entries.title: Germany benefits from work with Ukraine — defense minister
entries.snippet: The German defense minister said Ukraine was quick to develop new, battlefield-tested technologies. And the German government announced several measures to increase defense cooperation between the two countries.

entries.title: Germany: Stranded whale 'Timmy' being transported towards ocean in special barge
entries.snippet: Rescue teams are celebrating what they hope is a major win weeks into efforts to assist a stranded humpback whale. "Timmy" has been coaxed onto a special barge that will help steer him back towards the Atlantic.

entries.title: What Renault's new EV reveals about the global auto industry
entries.snippet: French carmaker Renault no longer sells its brand in the Chinese market, but it's not afraid to design and develop them there. It's just one more sign of big shifts in the global auto industry.

entries.title: Germany's birth rate reached lowest level on record in 2025
entries.snippet: As the number of women of childbearing age declines in Germany, birth rates are projected to continue falling. The German Family Association says family-unfriendly structural policies are to blame.

entries.title: King Charles addresses joint meeting of US Congress
entries.snippet: King Charles becomes the second British monarch to address both chambers of the US Congress. Charles and his wife, Queen Camilla, are visiting Washington for an official state visit.

entries.title: Iran war: Who in Tehran could shape talks with the US?
entries.snippet: It is unclear how negotiations between the US and Iran will proceed, and a breakthrough seems far off. DW looks at who is calling the shots in Tehran.

entries.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy rebukes Israel for buying grain 'stolen' by Russia
entries.snippet: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has accused Israel of "purchasing stolen goods," namely grain from regions occupied by Russia. Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador on Monday, and now it says another such ship has docked.

entries.title: UAE quits OPEC in shock move amid energy turmoil
entries.snippet: The United Arab Emirates is withdrawing from the OPEC and OPEC+ oil cartels as the Iran war drives a historic energy shock and rattles the global economy.

entries.title: Nigeria's leader battles security, political crises
entries.snippet: As politicians gear up for the 2027 elections, millions of Nigerians face a worsening security situation. Analysts say President Tinubu's recent cabinet reshuffle aims to rebuild public trust.

entries.title: Japan looks to woo allies with new weapons deals
entries.snippet: Tokyo has scrapped a decades-old ban on exporting lethal weapons, aiming to bolster allies as security tensions with China and North Korea intensify.

entries.title: Greek police hunt for alleged 89-year-old shooter on the run
entries.snippet: Greek police are looking for a gunman, reportedly aged 89, who opened fire on a social security office and a courthouse in central Athens, wounding several, local media reports say.

entries.title: South Korea: Ex-first lady Kim handed longer graft sentence
entries.snippet: Kim Keon Hee, wife of Yoon Suk Yeol, now faces a four-year jail sentence on charges of stock manipulation and bribery. The court also found she accepted gifts from a contentious church, knowing it expected favors.

entries.title: Austrian man admits plot to attack 2024 Taylor Swift concert
entries.snippet: An Austrian citizen accused of planning an attack at one of Taylor Swift's concerts in Vienna in 2024 pleaded guilty as his trial opened on Tuesday. The man was also tried on various terrorism charges.

entries.title: Fact check: Cloud seeding didn't make it rain in Iran
entries.snippet: Some social media users have claimed the alleged destruction of a climate research center in the United Arab Emirates caused intense precipitation in Iran. But those claims are misleading.

entries.title: Bundesliga games abroad unlikely, but for how long?
entries.snippet: New proposals from FIFA will allow domestic leagues to host one game abroad a season. As a result, Europe's football leagues could face a big change sooner rather than later.

entries.title: Iran war: US wary of peace plan postponing nuclear deal
entries.snippet: The White House has reportedly not ruled out a proposal from Iran to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts a blockade. However, US officials say any agreement must include a nuclear deal. DW has more.
